primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer, au regard d’IFRS 9, ce que signifie le critère des flux contractuels constitués exclusivement de remboursements de principal et d’intérêts pour la classification d’un actif financier.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that an IFRS standard supersedes an IAS standard where both touch the same issue; IFRS 9 directly governs the contractual cash flow characteristics test for financial asset classification, while IAS 32 only provides definitional context.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — IFRS 9 explicitly contains the SPPI test and the related classification models for financial assets.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly define the SPPI test and link it to the classification and measurement models for financial assets.

supporting_authority.reason: This provides the definition of a financial asset, which is background context for the IFRS 9 classification analysis.

peripheral_authority.reason: This addresses service concession receivables and points back to IFRS 9, but does not help identify peer approaches for the SPPI issue itself.
These paragraphs mention financial instruments in employee benefit plan contexts but are unrelated to the SPPI classification question.
These paragraphs address classification issues in business combinations and contingent consideration, not the meaning of SPPI for financial assets.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding
Financial assets whose contractual cash flows are not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes amortised cost as a top-level classification model available only when the asset meets the SPPI condition and the relevant business model condition.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes fair value through other comprehensive income as a top-level classification model for debt-type financial assets that meet the SPPI condition and the relevant business model condition.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 uses fair value through profit or loss as the residual top-level classification model when the asset does not satisfy the SPPI condition or otherwise does not qualify for the other debt measurement models.
